content:
# Gasmann Modeling

**Summary**: A model used to calculate the effect of fluid substitution on rock properties like bulk modulus and velocity.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2016-ali|Ali, 2016]] applied Gassmann's equation for fluid substitution to determine how gas, oil, and brine affect seismic properties.

- [[2019-al-dawood|Al-Dawood, 2019]] utilized Gassmann's fluid substitution equation for gas fluid substitution.

- [[2020-almumtin|Almumtin, 2020]] applied gasmann-modeling to bring data to a common brine saturation.

- [[2023-alsahwan|AlSahwan, 2023]] utilized Gassmann modeling to calculate bulk and shear moduli for different fluid saturations.

## Scientific Context

- [[2016-ali|Ali, 2016]] Used to model how different pore fluids change rock bulk modulus and velocity.

- [[2019-al-dawood|Al-Dawood, 2019]] utilized Gassmann's fluid substitution equation for gas fluid substitution.

- [[2020-almumtin|Almumtin, 2020]] applied gasmann-modeling to bring data to a common brine saturation.

- [[2023-alsahwan|AlSahwan, 2023]] utilized Gassmann modeling to calculate bulk and shear moduli for different fluid saturations.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2016-ali|Ali, 2016]] used for fluid substitution in the study's models

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Gassmann's equation is well known for fluid substitution with some assumptions and it is used for fluid substitution in this research. ([[2016-ali|Ali, 2016, p. 13]])

- Used to determine pay zones and feasibility through rock physics modeling. ([[2019-al-dawood|Al-Dawood, 2019, p. 15]])

- Gassmann's theory is applicable at both wireline and seismic frequency ranges. ([[2020-almumtin|Almumtin, 2020, p. 34]])

- Used to construct the VP (SW) relation by accounting for mineralogy and pore-fluid properties. ([[2023-alsahwan|AlSahwan, 2023, p. 26]])
